title: Expense Policy
content:
# Expense Policy

## Allowed Expenses

* Business travel
* Client meetings
* Conferences

## Submission Process

* Submit expenses via the Finance Portal
* Attach receipts for all expenses

## Approval Rules

* Expenses under $100 → automatic approval
* Expenses over $100 → manager approval required

## Reimbursement

* Processed within 5 business days after approval

title: Vacation Policy
content:
# Vacation Policy

## Overview

Employees are entitled to 25 days of paid vacation per year.

## Request Process

* Submit vacation request via the HR portal
* Requests must be submitted at least 5 business days in advance
* Manager approval is required

## Extended Leave

* For absences longer than 3 business days:

* additional approval from department lead is required

## Carry Over

* Up to 5 unused days can be carried over to the next year

## Notes

title: Employee Onboarding
content:
# Employee Onboarding

## First Day Checklist

* Access company email
* Set up development environment
* Join Slack workspace

## Accounts

* GitHub
* Jira
* HR Portal

## Training

* Complete security training within first week
* Review engineering guidelines

## Contacts

* HR: [hr@company.internal](mailto:hr@company.internal)
* IT Support: [it@company.internal](mailto:it@company.internal)

title: Incident Response
content:
# Incident Response

## Definition

An incident is any event that disrupts service availability.

## Steps

1. Identify issue
2. Notify team in Slack #incidents
3. Assign incident owner
4. Mitigate issue
5. Write postmortem

## Severity Levels

* SEV1: full outage
* SEV2: partial degradation
* SEV3: minor issue

## Postmortem

Must be completed within 48 hours

title: Local Development Setup
content:
# Local Development Setup

## Requirements

* Docker
* Rust (latest stable)
* Node.js (optional)

## Setup Steps

1. Clone repository
2. Run `docker compose up`
3. Verify services:

* host: http://localhost:3000
  * tools: http://localhost:3001

## Notes

* Use `.env` file for configuration
* Logs are available via `docker compose logs`

title: Architecture Overview
content:
# Architecture Overview

## Components

* Rust Host (API + orchestration)
* MCP Tools Server
* LLM Backend (Ollama / vLLM)
* Retrieval Layer (RAG)

## Flow

User → Host → LLM → Tools / Retrieval → Response

## Notes

* System is modular
* Backends are configurable via environment variables

title: Release Process
content:
# Release Process

## Steps

1. Merge PR to main
2. Run CI pipeline
3. Build Docker images
4. Deploy to staging
5. Run tests
6. Deploy to production

## Rollback

* Use previous stable Docker image

title: Support Guidelines
content:
# Support Guidelines

## Response Time

* Critical: 1 hour
* High: 4 hours
* Normal: 24 hours

## Channels

* Slack support channel
* Email support

## Escalation

* Escalate to engineering if unresolved after 24 hours

title: Product FAQ
content:
# Product FAQ

## What is this system?

An internal AI assistant for company knowledge and workflows.

## What can it do?

* Answer questions about company policies
* Retrieve documentation
* Call internal tools

## Limitations

* May hallucinate if data is missing
* Depends on available tools and documents
